channel.title: Cookings on Fernando Simões
channel.description: Recent content in Cookings on Fernando Simões

item.title: Testing software
item.description: I’ve been working in software development for almost 4 years, and although it’s not an extensive amount of time, I don’t think I’ve ever met a developer who liked to write tests. In fact, the tooling we have seems to confirm and endorse this sentiment. It’s hard to write tests against services running on different nodes; it’s hard to write tests against integration functionality. Mocking, faking, the London school, all these things don’t bring us glory in this ungrateful field.

A few months back I encountered an interesting behavior: I was trying to debug a legacy Flask API running on Docker. Since I had no time to setup a proper debugger, I began to add print statements to the backend code (don’t judge me, I bet you are lazy too). The problem was: the print output was inconsistent: I tried to reload the React frontend once, and nothing appeared. Then I reload again.
